{"id":"project-LICHEN-hkim","title":"TUI _connect_to_sim doesn't close SimRadio on connection failure","description":"file: python/src/lichen/tui/app.py:329-358

When _connect_to_sim catches an exception from SimRadio.connect(), it sets self._radio = None without first calling close() on the SimRadio object. If SimRadio.__init__ or the early part of connect() opened any resources (like sockets), those resources may leak.

Current code:
  try:
      self._radio = SimRadio(...)
      await self._radio.connect()
      ...
  except SimRadioError as e:
      self._log_event('error', f'Connection failed: {e}')
      self._radio = None  # Resources not closed

Should be:
  except SimRadioError as e:
      self._log_event('error', f'Connection failed: {e}')
      if self._radio is not None:
          with suppress(Exception):
              await self._radio.close()
      self._radio = None","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T17:06:38.809043Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T17:06:38.809078Z"}
